revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Juzen-no-mori Kofun]][[ja:十善の森古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Juzen-no-mori Kofun]][[ja:十善の森古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Juzen-no-mori Kofun]][[ja:十善の森古墳]] [[ja:十善の森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.4644|N|135.846044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Juzen-no-Mori Kofun''' (十善の森古墳, also spelled 十善ノ森古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui|en|Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui|qid=Q1204541|lt=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}, Fukui Prefecture. It has a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped) design and is part of the Kami-Chū Kofun Group, specifically the Tentokuji Kofun Cluster. The site is designated as a Prefectural Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Fukui Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The Juzen-no-Mori Kofun is located in the southwestern part of Fukui Prefecture, within the central Wakasa region. It was constructed on the alluvial fan at the foot of Tentokuji Mountain, along the left bank of the {{ill|Kita_River|en|Kita_River|qid=Q11401571|lt=Kita River|1=Kita River}}.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} The kofun belongs to the Tentokuji Kofun Cluster, which is part of the larger Kami-Chū Kofun Group, encompassing the Wakibukuro and Nikasa clusters. Other nearby mounds include the {{ill|Maruyama-zuka Kofun (Wakasa)|lt=Maruyama-zuka Kofun (Wakasa)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Wakasa)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (若狭町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Wakasa)|qq=}} (no longer extant) and the Kamitakano Kofun.{{Harvid|Exhibit Catalog|2014}} The site has undergone transformations due to quarrying and erosion, with parts of the mound altered by excavation.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Archaeological investigations were conducted in 1954 and again from 1994 to 1995.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} The mound is oriented westward, with a two-tiered construction. Outer features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments, with evidence suggesting the presence of a {{ill|tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} platform on the northern constricted portion of the mound.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} Surrounding the mound is a moat, with a land bridge detected in its southwestern portion. Artifacts recovered include decorative objects of Korean Peninsula origin, further indicating the region’s interaction with ancient states such as Baekje and Gaya. This kofun is dated to the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, or possibly the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} It is considered part of the burial lineage of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), a ruling family in the Wakasa region.{{Harvid|Kami-Chū Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Burial Facilities == The mound contains two {{ill|yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers), located in the circular rear and rectangular front portions. === Rear Chamber === The rear chamber shows a design influenced by northern Kyushu styles, with a left-sided offset entrance. It is oriented southward and features red ochre on its interior walls. The burial floor is composed of river pebbles.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} Dimensions include: * '''Main chamber''': 4.1–4.4m in length, 1.5m at the base, 2.1m at the rear wall, and 2.7m in height. * '''Entrance''': 0.6m wide and 1.15m high. * '''Ante-chamber''': 1.85m long and 2.85m at its widest point. Artifacts recovered here include jewelry, horse trappings, and ceremonial weapons.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Continued Kofun)|2002}} === Front Chamber === The front chamber consists of a smaller offset stone chamber, likely constructed later. Red ochre was also applied to its walls.{{Harvid|Juzen-no-Mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Fukui Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Juzen-no-Mori Kofun Artifacts (archaeological materials)''': Includes numerous grave goods, designated on July 25, 1978.{{Harvid|Cultural Properties List}} * '''Juzen-no-Mori Kofun''': Designated as a historic site on October 11, 1978.{{Harvid|Fukui Prefecture}} Artifacts include: * Chinese-made mirrors. * Baekje-style gold crowns and fittings. * Gaya-style decorative horse equipment. * Beads, pendants, and other jewelry. == Dimensions == The mound measures as follows: * Total length: 67 meters. * Rear circular portion: 33 meters in diameter. * Front rectangular portion: 45 meters wide. File:Juzen-no-mori Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|View from the south.
{{small|The road visible to the far right is National Route 27.}} File:Juzen-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Rear circular portion (viewed from the front). File:Juzen-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Front rectangular portion (viewed from the rear). == Related Facilities == * '''Wakasa History and Culture Museum (Wakasa-cho Ichiba)''': Houses and displays artifacts from the Juzen-no-Mori Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=日本歴史地名大系 18 福井県の地名|publisher=平凡社|year=1981|isbn=4582490182}} * {{Cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|editor=原田道雄|publisher=東京堂出版|year=1989|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|title=続 日本古墳大辞典|editor=鈴木篤英|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002|isbn=4490105991}} == External Links == * [http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tentokuji-juzennomori.html Juzen-no-Mori Kofun - Fukui Prefecture Cultural Heritage Site] * [http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kouko/wakasacho-shishinkyo.html Artifacts from Juzen-no-Mori Kofun] {{translated page|ja|十善の森古墳|version=100187145|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:十善の森古墳]] {{Commons category|Juzen-no-mori Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279193}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakasa, Fukui]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukui prefecture]] [[Category:Wakasa, Fukui]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zen Clan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
